evidence: The paper uses blocked permutation importance ("we constructed two blocked permutation importance measures also based on the R^2"), measured on 60/40 held-out test splits. Results report magnitudes and rankings: "M zyg = .110, M corr = .108" and "In 7 out of 13 appraisal dimensions, the zygomaticus channel showed the highest importance value." However, these conclusions are corroborated by ALE plots showing actual relationships, model comparison (RF>RIDGE indicating nonlinearity), and out-of-sample validation. Since the paper uses permutation importance rather than tree impurity importance, the impurity importance bias does not apply.
